scenarios.title: Image Generation and LoRA Training Workflows
scenarios.when_to_read: Stable Diffusion WebUI launch, API automation, extension scripting, model asset management, Lora/extra networks, textual inversion, hypernetwork training, extras, and postprocessing tasks.
scenarios.role: Provides self-contained operational guidance for AUTOMATIC1111 Stable Diffusion WebUI workflows across launch/configuration, REST APIs, extensions, assets, and training/postprocessing.
scenarios.read_when: User mentions stable-diffusion-webui, AUTOMATIC1111, WebUI, launch.py, webui.sh, /sdapi/v1, txt2img/img2img API payloads, alwayson_scripts, checkpoints, VAE, Lora, embeddings, hypernetworks, Extras, textual inversion, or WebUI extensions.
scenarios.best_for: Running or configuring WebUI, constructing API requests, writing WebUI scripts/extensions, validating model asset layouts, managing Lora/extra networks, and planning WebUI training/postprocessing workflows.
scenarios.avoid_when: Use Diffusers-specific skills for Python pipeline APIs outside WebUI, ComfyUI-specific skills for node graphs, or generic repository maintenance skills when the task is only source editing without WebUI domain behavior.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose `stable-diffusion-webui` when the practical surface is AUTOMATIC1111 Stable Diffusion WebUI. Prefer `launch-and-config` for flags/server startup, `api-automation` for /sdapi payloads, `assets-and-models` for checkpoints/VAEs/Lora/upscalers, `extension-scripting` for scripts/callbacks, and `training-and-postprocessing` for textual inversion, hypernetworks, Extras, and preprocessing.

scenarios.title: Python AI API Services and Deployment
scenarios.when_to_read: Stable Diffusion WebUI is being exposed as an authenticated API service, reverse-proxied behind a subpath, controlled through FastAPI routes, or diagnosed as a local AI service.
scenarios.role: Covers WebUI-specific API service launch, authentication, CORS/TLS/subpath flags, /sdapi route behavior, and server lifecycle hazards.
scenarios.read_when: User asks about --api, --nowebui, --api-auth, --listen, --subpath, CORS, TLS, /sdapi/v1 routes, WebUI API payloads, API errors, or server-stop/restart endpoints.
scenarios.best_for: WebUI API deployment and automation tasks where WebUI-specific flags and /sdapi schemas matter more than generic FastAPI patterns.
scenarios.avoid_when: Use generic FastAPI/deployment skills for non-WebUI services or AI gateway skills for provider routing/proxy infrastructure.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Route WebUI service exposure through launch-and-config first, then api-automation for request construction and endpoint troubleshooting.

scenarios.title: Python Repository Maintenance
scenarios.when_to_read: The user is modifying or auditing the stable-diffusion-webui checkout and needs repo-specific source, tests, scripts, extension APIs, or safe static-inspection helpers.
scenarios.role: Provides repo-specific maps of launch, API, extension, asset, and training modules plus safe bundled helpers for source auditing without starting WebUI.
scenarios.read_when: User asks to edit WebUI source, inspect cmd_args.py, inspect modules/api/api.py, update extension callbacks, adapt built-in scripts, or understand native tests in the checkout.
scenarios.best_for: Repository changes that require Stable Diffusion WebUI domain context before editing or testing.
scenarios.avoid_when: Use a generic Python maintenance skill when the task is formatting, linting, packaging, or CI plumbing with no WebUI-specific behavior.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Use the relevant sub-skill to understand the domain surface, then apply normal repo-maintenance practices for code edits and tests.
